fault_hypothesis: The condition for clamping the result is inverted on line 63. The code enters the clamping logic when `clamp` is `False` and skips it when `clamp` is `True`, which is the opposite of the documented behavior.

constraints.intent: All failing tests involve extrapolation (t is outside [0,1]), which this conditional should handle, but its logic is inverted.
When clamp=True (this branch is taken), an extrapolated value should be clamped, but here the value is out of bounds and is not being modified.
When clamp=False (this branch is taken), the value should not be clamped, but this code incorrectly modifies the extrapolated value y__1.
